Requirement.Name: Requirement Typing Consistency
Requirement.Content: The system shall classify each requirement as either a Functional Requirement (FR) or a Non-Functional Requirement (NFR), and shall not use combined types such as "NFR / FR".

Requirement.Name: Exploration State Machine
Requirement.Content: The system shall implement an exploration state machine with, at minimum, the following states: IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, and ERROR, and shall transition between these states based on exploration progress and fault conditions.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Start Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall enter the EXPLORE state when the robot is powered on, initialized, and a start signal is received from a predefined trigger (e.g., user button press or software command).

Requirement.Name: Unknown Cell Selection
Requirement.Content: While in the EXPLORE state, the system shall periodically select a target cell that is currently marked as UNKNOWN in the internal maze representation and is reachable from the robot's current position.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Path Planning
Requirement.Content: For each selected UNKNOWN target cell, the system shall compute a collision-free path from the robot's current position to the target using the current internal maze representation.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Termination Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall terminate the EXPLORE state and mark exploration as done when either (a) no UNKNOWN cells are reachable from the robot's current position or (b) the configured exploration time budget is reached.

Requirement.Name: Continuous Wall Avoidance During Exploration
Requirement.Content: While in the EXPLORE state, the system shall continuously adjust its motion commands such that the robot remains within a configured safety distance from any detected wall, overriding planned motion commands when necessary to avoid collision.

Requirement.Name: Wall Detection From Sensors
Requirement.Content: The system shall interpret distance or proximity readings from wall-related sensors to detect the presence of walls within a configurable detection range in front of and to the sides of the robot.

Requirement.Name: Wall Proximity Thresholds
Requirement.Content: The system shall maintain at least two configurable distance thresholds for each wall sensor direction: a warning distance and a hard-stop distance, and shall treat readings below these thresholds as proximity and collision risk respectively.

Requirement.Name: Reactive Obstacle Avoidance
Requirement.Content: When a wall is detected within the warning distance, the system shall modify the current motion commands to steer away from the wall, and when a wall is detected within the hard-stop distance, the system shall immediately command the actuators to stop forward motion.

Requirement.Name: Wall Detection Update Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall process wall-related sensor data and update wall detection and avoidance decisions at a fixed rate of at least 20 Hz.

Requirement.Name: Maze Grid Representation
Requirement.Content: The system shall represent the maze as a two-dimensional grid of discrete cells, where each cell stores at least its classification (FREE, BLOCKED, UNKNOWN) and its occupancy update state.

Requirement.Name: Coordinate Frame for Map
Requirement.Content: The system shall define a fixed map coordinate frame with the origin at the robot's start position and shall consistently map robot pose estimates to grid cell indices in this frame.

Requirement.Name: Robot Pose Tracking
Requirement.Content: The system shall maintain an estimate of the robot's current pose (position and orientation) in the map frame by integrating odometry and other relevant sensor inputs.

Requirement.Name: Map Update From Sensor Data
Requirement.Content: The system shall update the grid cell classifications based on incoming range or contact sensor data by projecting sensor measurements into the map frame using the current robot pose.

Requirement.Name: Cell Classification States
Requirement.Content: The system shall support at least three cell states: FREE for traversable space, BLOCKED for walls or obstacles, and UNKNOWN for cells that have not yet been observed.

Requirement.Name: Classification From Wall Detection
Requirement.Content: When a wall is detected along a sensor beam at a given distance, the system shall classify the corresponding grid cells up to the wall as FREE and the cell at the wall location as BLOCKED.

Requirement.Name: Unknown Cell Initialization
Requirement.Content: Upon system initialization, the system shall set the classification state of all cells within the configured maze bounds to UNKNOWN.

Requirement.Name: Explored Cell Determination
Requirement.Content: The system shall treat any cell whose classification has changed from UNKNOWN to either FREE or BLOCKED as explored for the purpose of exploration progress tracking.

Requirement.Name: Mapping Time Budget
Requirement.Content: The system shall enforce a maximum elapsed time of 5 minutes from exploration start to exploration termination for completing the mapping of the maze.

Requirement.Name: Time Tracking Mechanism
Requirement.Content: The system shall track the elapsed mapping time using a monotonic system clock with a resolution of at most 10 milliseconds.

Requirement.Name: Graceful Timeout Handling
Requirement.Content: When the 5-minute mapping time budget is exceeded, the system shall stop exploring, preserve the current internal map, and transition to the RETURN_TO_START state without disabling collision avoidance.

Requirement.Name: Grid-Based Path Planner
Requirement.Content: The system shall compute paths on the internal grid representation using only cells classified as FREE and shall treat BLOCKED cells as non-traversable.

Requirement.Name: Path Optimality Criterion
Requirement.Content: The system shall define path optimality as minimizing the total path length measured as the sum of Euclidean or Manhattan distances between successive waypoints, depending on the configured grid connectivity.

Requirement.Name: Shortest Path Algorithm
Requirement.Content: The system shall use a complete shortest-path search algorithm, such as A* or Dijkstra's Algorithm, to compute an optimal path on the grid from a specified start cell to a specified target cell.

Requirement.Name: Handling Unreachable Targets
Requirement.Content: If the planner cannot find a collision-free path to the requested target cell, the system shall report the target as unreachable and trigger selection of an alternative reachable target or terminate exploration based on current state.

Requirement.Name: Path Replanning on Map Updates
Requirement.Content: When new map information changes the classification of any cell on the current planned path to BLOCKED, the system shall trigger replanning of the path to the current target from the robot's current position.

Requirement.Name: Start Pose Recording
Requirement.Content: At system initialization, the system shall record the robot's start pose in the map frame and use it as the return-to-start target position.

Requirement.Name: Return Path Planning
Requirement.Content: When exploration is marked as done, the system shall compute a path from the robot's current pose to the recorded start pose using the internal maze representation.

Requirement.Name: Return Path Execution
Requirement.Content: The system shall command the actuators to follow the computed path back to the start pose while continuing to apply wall detection and collision avoidance.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Initialization
Requirement.Content: After successfully reaching the start pose at the end of exploration, the system shall transition from RETURN_TO_START to SECOND_RUN state without resetting the internal maze representation.

Requirement.Name: Target Selection for Second Run
Requirement.Content: At the beginning of the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall select a predefined target cell within the maze, such as the maze goal cell, based on configuration or competition rules.

Requirement.Name: Efficient Path Computation for Second Run
Requirement.Content: Before starting motion in the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall compute the shortest path from the start pose to the selected target cell using the final internal maze representation created during exploration.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Path Execution
Requirement.Content: During the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall send actuator commands to follow the precomputed most efficient path to the target while still enforcing collision avoidance behavior.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Completion
Requirement.Content: The system shall declare the second run complete when the robot pose is within a configured position and orientation tolerance of the target cell center.

Requirement.Name: No Contact With Walls
Requirement.Content: The system shall select proximity and hard-stop thresholds and corresponding motion commands such that, under nominal sensor and actuator behavior, the robot chassis shall remain at least a configured minimum nonzero distance away from any wall.

Requirement.Name: Collision Avoidance Priority
Requirement.Content: In case of conflict between goal-directed motion and collision avoidance, the system shall prioritize collision avoidance and may temporarily deviate from the planned path.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Plausibility Checks
Requirement.Content: The system shall validate incoming sensor data using plausibility checks such as range limits, rate-of-change limits, and cross-sensor consistency before using the data for mapping, localization, or collision avoidance.

Requirement.Name: Faulty Sensor Data Handling
Requirement.Content: When sensor data is detected as faulty by plausibility checks, the system shall discard or down-weight the data and continue operating using remaining valid sensor inputs and the current internal state estimate.

Requirement.Name: Degraded Mode Operation
Requirement.Content: If one or more critical sensors are persistently faulty beyond a configurable duration, the system shall enter a degraded mode in which it reduces speed and restricts motion to previously mapped FREE cells only.

Requirement.Name: Safe Stop on Severe Fault
Requirement.Content: When the system detects a severe sensor or actuator fault that prevents reliable collision avoidance, it shall command the actuators to stop motion and remain stationary until reset.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Ingestion
Requirement.Content: The system shall receive sensor data messages as specified in the sensor data interface description and store them in an internal data structure with timestamps for subsequent processing.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Synchronization
Requirement.Content: The system shall synchronize data from multiple sensors based on their timestamps to produce coherent sensor frames for mapping, localization, and collision avoidance computations.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Processing Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall process incoming sensor data at a configurable rate of at least 20 Hz to support real-time navigation and mapping.

Requirement.Name: Actuator Command Output
Requirement.Content: The system shall send motor control commands that conform to the motor interface description, including speed, direction, and duration or position parameters as required.

Requirement.Name: Motor Command Update Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall update actuator commands at a fixed rate of at least 20 Hz while the robot is in motion.

Requirement.Name: Velocity and Turning Control
Requirement.Content: The system shall convert planned linear and angular velocities into appropriate motor interface commands to achieve forward motion, backward motion, and in-place or curving turns as required by the navigation subsystem.

Requirement.Name: Emergency Stop Command
Requirement.Content: The system shall support an emergency stop command that immediately sets all motor speed commands to zero and is issued when a hard-stop wall proximity condition or severe fault condition is detected.
